title: Script This: Med Spa
hook: Med spas spend $5,000 a month on ads. I'm about to write them a script for free that'll outperform all of it.
topic: Showing how to script fear-based med spa content that sells without selling — the client story format.

script.label: HOOK
script.text: Med spas spend $5,000 a month on ads. I'm about to write them a script for free that'll outperform all of it.

script.label: BODY 1
script.text: Here's what every med spa gets wrong with their content. They post before-and-afters with zero context. No story, no emotion, just a side-by-side pic and 'book now.' That doesn't make anyone feel anything.

script.label: REHOOK
script.text: Here's what actually works—

script.label: BODY 2
script.text:
Hook: 'She almost canceled her appointment because she was terrified of looking fake.'

Body: 'This is Sarah. She wanted Botox for a year but kept scrolling horror stories online. Frozen faces. People who don't look like themselves anymore. So she almost didn't come in. But here's what we told her — good Botox doesn't make you look different. It makes you look like you slept 8 hours. Watch her reaction seeing herself for the first time.'

Then you show the reveal. That's it. That's the whole video.

script.label: BODY 3
script.text: You're not selling Botox. You're selling the relief that it won't ruin your face. Every med spa client has that fear. Address the fear, show the outcome, let the emotion do the selling.

script.label: OUTRO
script.text: If you run a med spa and your content isn't doing this — your content isn't working. Drop your business in the comments, I'll tell you what to post.

value: Reveals the psychology behind med spa buying decisions — fear vs. desire. Shows the exact content format that works.
cta: What treatment would YOU want to see scripted? 👇

title: Script This: Attorney
hook: Lawyers think they can't go viral. I wrote a personal injury attorney a script in under a minute that would get more leads than a billboard.
topic: Scripting a PI attorney using the 'expose the threat' format — building trust by giving away insider knowledge.

script.label: HOOK
script.text: Lawyers think they can't go viral. I wrote a personal injury attorney a script in under a minute that would get more leads than a billboard.

script.label: BODY 1
script.text: Every PI attorney's content is the same. Guy in a suit pointing at text on screen: 'Were you injured in a car accident? Call us today.' Nobody stops for that. You know why? Because it sounds like every other lawyer. And people don't trust lawyers — that's the real problem you need to solve first.

script.label: REHOOK
script.text: So here's the script—

script.label: BODY 2
script.text:
Hook: 'The insurance company is hoping you don't watch this video.'

Body: 'If you just got in a car accident, the insurance adjuster is going to call you within 48 hours. They're going to sound really nice. They're going to ask how you're feeling. And then they're going to offer you a check. Do NOT take it. That first offer? It's designed to be as low as possible before you realize how bad your injuries actually are. Here's exactly what to say when they call—'

Then you give them the script. Word for word. Free.

script.label: BODY 3
script.text: You're not saying 'hire me.' You're giving them something they desperately need in a moment of panic. That builds more trust than any billboard ever could. And when they DO need a lawyer? You're the one who helped them when nobody else did.

script.label: OUTRO
script.text: If you're an attorney and your content is just you pointing at text — we need to talk. Drop your practice area below and I'll script it.

value: Shows how attorneys can build trust through radical transparency — giving away insider knowledge builds authority faster than any ad.

title: The Script Template
hook: I'm about to give you the exact script template I use for every single client. Screenshot this.
topic: Giving away the 5-line fill-in-the-blank script template — the highest save-bait content possible.

script.label: HOOK
script.text: I'm about to give you the exact script template I use for every single client. Screenshot this.

script.label: BODY 1
script.text: Every viral business video follows the same structure. It doesn't matter if you're a dentist, a roofer, or a fitness coach. The framework is the same. And most people overcomplicate it.

script.label: REHOOK
script.text: Here's the template. Ready?

script.label: BODY 2
script.text:
Line one — the hook: '[Your customer's biggest fear] is costing you [dollar amount or consequence].'

Line two — the problem: 'Most [type of business] try to fix this by [common wrong approach]. But that actually makes it worse because [reason].'

Line three — the solution: 'Here's what actually works: [your unique approach in one sentence].'

Line four — the proof: 'We did this for [client type] and [specific result with a number].'

Line five — the CTA: 'If you want the same thing, [simple next step].'

That's it. Five lines. Fill in the blanks. You have a script.

script.label: BODY 3
script.text: This template works because it follows how people actually make decisions. Fear, problem, solution, proof, action. In that order. Don't rearrange it. Don't add fluff. Just fill in the blanks and hit record.

script.label: OUTRO
script.text: Save this. Use it. If it works — and it will — come back and tell me.

value: Gives away the actual tool — the fill-in-the-blank script framework used for every CMP client. Highest save potential of any content we've made.
cta: Save 🔖 + tag someone who needs this

title: Why Your Content Feels Off
hook: You're posting consistently, getting decent views, but no one's reaching out. Here's why.
topic: Diagnosing the most common content strategy mistake — making content about yourself instead of your customer's fear.

script.label: HOOK
script.text: You're posting consistently, getting decent views, but no one's reaching out. Here's why.

script.label: BODY 1
script.text: Your content is entertaining but it doesn't position you as the solution to a problem. You've got attention but no direction. People watch, they like, they move on. They never think 'I should hire this person.'

script.label: REHOOK
script.text: And it comes down to one missing piece—

script.label: BODY 2
script.text:
Every piece of content needs to answer one question for the viewer: 'What do I do next?' Not with a 'link in bio' or 'DM me.' With value so specific that they think 'this person clearly knows my problem better than I do.'

When a roofer posts 'We do great roofs!' that's nice. When a roofer posts 'Your roof is leaking in a spot you can't see — here's how to check in 30 seconds' — now they're the expert. Now there's a reason to call THEM.

script.label: BODY 3
script.text: Stop making content about what you do. Start making content about what your customer is afraid of. That's the shift.

script.label: OUTRO
script.text: If you're posting and not getting leads — the content isn't broken. The strategy is.

value: Diagnoses the exact frustration most business owners feel — posting without results. Names the real cause and gives the fix in one sentence.
cta: What's your business? I'll tell you what to post 👇
caption:
You're posting. Getting views. Nobody's reaching out.

Here's the actual problem:

Your content is entertaining. It's not positioning.

People watch, they like, they scroll.
They never think 'I need to hire this person.'

The fix:

Stop making content about what YOU do.
Start making content about what your customer is AFRAID of.

That one shift changes everything.

title: The Vanity Metric Trap
hook: If your content is getting 10,000 views and you still have no clients — it's not the algorithm. You're measuring the wrong thing.
topic: Exposing vanity metrics (views) vs. conversion metrics (saves, shares) — the real scorecard for business content.

script.label: HOOK
script.text: If your content is getting 10,000 views and you still have no clients — it's not the algorithm. You're measuring the wrong thing.

script.label: BODY 1
script.text: You're watching views go up. Followers ticking. Telling yourself it's working. But your DMs are empty. Your phone isn't ringing. You're measuring vanity metrics. And vanity metrics pay nobody's bills.

script.label: REHOOK
script.text: Here's the thing most content coaches will never say to your face...

script.label: BODY 2
script.text:
Views are the audience's glance. Saves are their receipt. Shares are their referral. Those three — saves, shares, comments asking questions — that's your actual conversion funnel.

A post with 200 views and 4 saves will get you more clients than a post with 10K views and zero saves. I'd put money on it. Because saves mean: I need this. Shares mean: my friend needs this too. That's buyer behavior.

script.label: BODY 3
script.text: Stop asking how many people saw it. Start asking: did it make someone stop, save it, and think 'I need this person?' That is the metric that matters.

script.label: OUTRO
script.text: If you're posting content without tracking saves and shares — start today. That's your real scorecard. Everything else is noise.

value: Reframes how service business owners measure content success — shifts focus from vanity metrics to conversion indicators.
cta: Track saves. Not views.
caption:
Stop chasing views. Start tracking what actually drives clients. 📊

If your content isn't getting saves and shares, it's not converting — no matter what the view count says.

Views = glance
Saves = receipt
Shares = referral

That's your real funnel.

Drop a 🔖 if you needed to hear this.

title: Posting Consistently Is the Worst Advice
hook: Posting consistently is the worst advice anyone ever gave a business owner. And every marketing guru says it.
topic: Hot take — quality over quantity. One extraordinary post beats 30 mediocre ones.

script.label: HOOK
script.text: Posting consistently is the worst advice anyone ever gave a business owner. And every marketing guru says it.

script.label: BODY 1
script.text: Post every day. Stay consistent. Show up even when you don't feel like it. You've heard it. Most people follow it. And most people are posting garbage on a schedule — wondering why nothing's working. Consistency without strategy is just noise with a calendar.

script.label: REHOOK
script.text: Here's the thing...

script.label: BODY 2
script.text: One extraordinary post will outperform thirty mediocre ones. Every single time. One video that stops the scroll — says something nobody else has said — speaks directly to your buyer — that's worth more than a month of daily posts. The algorithm doesn't reward frequency. It rewards engagement. And engagement follows quality, not quantity.

script.label: BODY 3
script.text: Don't post more. Post better. Even if that means once a week. Make that one post so good it earns saves. Shares. DMs. Clients. Because garbage on a schedule is still garbage.

script.label: OUTRO
script.text: So before you stress about posting every day — ask yourself one question: Is my content actually good?

value: Gives exhausted business owners permission to slow down and focus on quality. Validates what they already suspect — frequency isn't the answer.
cta: Post better. Not more.
caption:
Hot take: 'Post consistently' has ruined more small businesses than it's helped.

One great post > 30 average ones.

Fight me in the comments. ⬇️

title: The AI Content Test
hook: There's a 3-second test that instantly tells me if content was written by AI. Most business owners are failing it right now.
topic: Exposing AI-generated content patterns vs. authentic human voice — a simple 3-second test any business owner can run on their own posts.

script.label: HOOK
script.text: There's a 3-second test that instantly tells me if content was written by AI. Most business owners are failing it right now.

script.label: BODY 1
script.text: The test is simple. Read the first sentence of your last post out loud. Does it sound like something you'd actually say to a client sitting across from you? Or does it sound like a LinkedIn post from 2019? AI writes in patterns. It uses phrases like 'in today's fast-paced world' and 'it's important to note that' and 'leverage your unique value proposition.' None of that sounds human. All of it kills trust instantly.

script.label: REHOOK
script.text: Here's the thing...

script.label: BODY 2
script.text: The problem isn't that AI writes bad content. The problem is AI writes content that sounds like every other business in your category. And when you sound like everyone else — you disappear. Your actual speaking voice is the differentiator. Quick fix: Record yourself answering one client question out loud. Transcribe it. That's your script. Messy, human, real. That's what works.

script.label: BODY 3
script.text: The businesses winning right now aren't the ones with the biggest budgets. They're the ones who sound like a person. That's it. That's the whole game.

script.label: OUTRO
script.text: Run your last 3 posts through this test right now. Read them out loud. If you'd never say it to a client's face — rewrite it. If you want help building a content voice that actually sounds like you? That's exactly what we do at CMP. Link's in bio.

value: Gives business owners a tangible, instant diagnostic for their AI-generated content problem. Positions authentic voice as the competitive advantage. Drives saves + shares from people who recognize the problem.
cta: Write how you TALK. Not how AI thinks you sound.

title: Going Viral Is the Most Expensive Way to Get Zero Clients
hook: Going viral is the most expensive way to get zero clients. I'm done pretending otherwise.
topic: Contrarian take: chasing virality is the wrong goal for service businesses — trust-building content for a targeted audience beats broad reach every time.

script.label: HOOK
script.text: Going viral is the most expensive way to get zero clients. I'm done pretending otherwise.

script.label: BODY 1
script.text: Everyone's chasing reach. Big numbers. Watch counts. And I get it — a viral video feels like winning. For a day. But viral content is built to talk to everyone. Which means it talks to no one who will actually hire you. The local business owner with 500 followers who posts specific, targeted content about their customer's exact problem? Booking clients every week. The guy with a viral post and 10,000 new followers? Not one of them is in his market.

script.label: REHOOK
script.text: Here's the thing...

script.label: BODY 2
script.text: Viral is built for audiences. Clients are built on trust. Those are two completely different content strategies. Trust content says: 'I understand your exact problem. I've solved it before. Here's proof.' Viral content says: 'Look at me.' One of those sentences leads to a sales conversation. The other gets a like and a scroll.

script.label: BODY 3
script.text: Stop optimizing for reach. Start optimizing for the right 50 people seeing your post and thinking — 'I need to call this person.' That's the only metric that pays your rent.

script.label: OUTRO
script.text: The goal isn't to go viral. The goal is to be unavoidable to the people who need what you do. One is ego. The other is a business. Drop a comment if you've been chasing the wrong number.

value: Gives exhausted, confused business owners permission to stop chasing vanity. Reframes the entire content goal. Highly shareable — business owners will send this to their partners, marketers, and social media managers.
cta: Relevant > Viral. Every time.

title: The First Client I Landed With Content
hook: The first client I ever landed from Instagram content was from a video I almost didn't post.
topic: First-person story of landing the first paying client through organic content — proving the system works, giving permission to post the 'obvious' thing.

script.label: HOOK
script.text: The first client I ever landed from Instagram content was from a video I almost didn't post.

script.label: BODY 1
script.text: It was a reel I filmed on my phone in my apartment. No set. No production. Just me talking about why most service businesses sound exactly the same on social media — and why that's quietly killing their leads. I almost didn't post it. I thought it was too basic. Too obvious. Like — who doesn't already know this?

script.label: REHOOK
script.text: Here's the thing...

script.label: BODY 2
script.text: I posted it anyway. Three days later, I got a DM from a business owner in Detroit. He said: 'You described my exact problem word for word. I didn't even know that was why my content wasn't working until I watched your video.' We had a call that week. He signed. And that taught me the most important lesson I know about content: obvious to you is not obvious to them. The stuff you think everyone already knows? That's exactly what someone is desperate to hear explained out loud.

script.label: BODY 3

title: The 90-Day Content Audit
hook: Every 90 days, I run my clients through a content audit. Most of them find out they've been wasting 60% of their time. Here's the exact process.
topic: A 4-step content audit that replaces guessing with a deliberate system — sort by saves, shares, dead weight, and content gaps.

script.label: HOOK
script.text: Every 90 days, I run my clients through a content audit. Most of them find out they've been wasting 60% of their time. Here's the exact process.

script.label: BODY 1
script.text: Here's what happens when you don't audit your content. You keep posting the same formats. The same topics. The same hooks that stopped working six months ago. You assume nothing's working because the algorithm hates you. But you've never actually looked at what IS working. You've been flying blind and calling it a strategy.

script.label: REHOOK
script.text: Here's the thing — the data already has your answers. You're just not reading it.

script.label: BODY 2
script.text:
The audit takes 20 minutes. Four steps.

Step one: Pull your last 90 days of content. Sort by saves. Not views — saves. The posts with the most saves are the posts your audience needed. That's your content goldmine.

Step two: Look at your top 3 by shares. Shares mean someone thought it was worth their reputation to send to another person. What format was it? What topic? That's your virality fingerprint.

Step three: Find your dead weight. Any post with less than half the engagement of your average? Kill that format. Don't post it again for 90 days.

Step four: Identify the gap. Look at what your audience saved and shared — then ask: what's the natural next question they'd have? That's your next 10 posts.

script.label: BODY 3
script.text: The businesses that win at content aren't the ones posting the most. They're the ones paying attention. Four steps, 20 minutes, every 90 days. That's all it takes to stop guessing and start building something deliberate.

script.label: OUTRO
script.text: Run this audit on your last 90 days right now. If you want the one-page version — drop AUDIT in the comments and I'll send it over.

script.text:
Show up. Stay consistent. Post every day even when you don't feel like it. That's the advice. And it comes from people who make content as the business. Content IS their product. Their payroll depends on making more content. Of course they post every day. They have nothing else to do.

But you? You have jobs to quote. Clients to service. Employees to manage. Invoices chasing. A business that requires your actual presence — not just your face on a phone screen.

script.text: Here's the thing...

script.label: BODY 2
script.text:
The advice isn't wrong for a content creator. It's completely wrong for a service business. And nobody in the 'just show up' camp is distinguishing between those two things.

You don't need to post every day. You need to post strategically. One post that speaks directly to your buyer's fear does more for your business than thirty posts of motivational Monday and birthday cakes.

title: The 3 Hook Formulas That Work for Any Business
hook: There are only 3 hook formulas that consistently work for service businesses on Instagram. After scripting 40+ clients — here they are.
topic: Three proven hook formulas that generate scroll-stopping content for any industry: Hidden Cost, Flip the Belief, and Specific Outcome Tease.

script.label: HOOK
script.text: There are only 3 hook formulas that consistently work for service businesses on Instagram. After scripting 40+ clients — here they are.

script.label: BODY 1
script.text: Most business owners approach their hooks the same way every time. They start with what they do, what they offer, or something generic like 'tips for small businesses.' Nobody stops for that. A hook has one job: interrupt the scroll. And it can only do that if it triggers one of three core emotional reactions in the first two seconds.

script.label: REHOOK
script.text: Here's the thing — every hook that stops a scroll is doing one of these three things.

script.label: BODY 2
script.text:
Formula One: Name the Hidden Cost. '[Blank] is quietly costing your business [consequence] — and you can't see it yet.' This works because it implies the viewer is losing something RIGHT NOW without knowing it. They can't not watch.

Formula Two: Flip the Common Belief. 'Everyone says [common advice]. It's wrong. Here's what actually works.' The brain is wired to resolve contradiction. If I believe something and you tell me it's wrong — I have to hear why.

Formula Three: The Specific Outcome Tease. 'I [did X specific thing] and [specific surprising result] happened within [specific timeframe].' Specificity is credibility. 'I posted once a week for 60 days and signed 3 clients' is infinitely more compelling than 'I grew my business with content.'

script.label: BODY 3
script.text: Every scroll-stopping hook you've ever seen is one of these three things. Hidden cost. Flipped belief. Specific outcome. Once you see it, you can't unsee it. And once you can build them intentionally — you stop guessing and start scripting.

script.label: OUTRO
script.text: Screenshot this. Use one of these formulas for your next post. Then come back and tell me which one you used and how it performed.

value: Extremely high save and share potential — concrete formulas the audience will want to keep. Positions Shota as the system guy, not just a content creator.
cta: 3 formulas. Every scroll-stopping hook comes from one of them.

title: The 3 Words That Kill Trust Instantly
hook: There are 3 phrases that instantly tell me your content wasn't written by you. And when your audience reads them — they bounce. Here's what they are.
topic: Three specific AI-generated phrases that instantly break reader trust — and the 30-second fix to audit your own content voice before posting.

script.label: HOOK
script.text: There are 3 phrases that instantly tell me your content wasn't written by you. And when your audience reads them — they bounce. Here's what they are.

script.label: BODY 1
script.text: Most business owners don't even realize they're doing it. They use AI to help draft their posts — which is totally fine — but then they publish the first draft without reading it out loud. So their Instagram sounds like a LinkedIn press release. The audience can't name exactly what's wrong. They just feel like something's off. There's a distance to it. So they scroll.

script.label: REHOOK
script.text: Here's the thing...

script.label: BODY 2
script.text: The three phrases. Number one: 'In today's world.' Empty. No human who actually runs a business starts a sentence that way. Number two: 'As a business owner, you know...' Nobody talks like that. Number three: 'Navigating the ever-changing landscape of...' That's a sentence that means absolutely nothing. If any of those phrases are in your content right now — that content is not in your voice. And a post that isn't in your voice will not build trust. Full stop. The fix takes 30 seconds. Read every post out loud before you publish. If you wouldn't say it to a client sitting across from you — rewrite it. That's the whole audit.

script.label: BODY 3
script.text: Your voice is your competitive advantage. The moment your content sounds like everyone else — you ARE everyone else. In a feed full of AI-generated posts in 2026, sounding like an actual person is the rarest thing you can post. It's also the most valuable.

script.label: OUTRO
script.text: Pull up your last 3 posts right now. Read them out loud. Count how many sentences you'd never actually say to someone's face. That number is your trust problem. Follow for the fix.

value: Extremely actionable. Gives a specific, memorable diagnostic anyone can run on their last post in 30 seconds. Screenshot-worthy. Builds on the AI content trend of 2026 authenticity crisis.
cta: Read it out loud. If you wouldn't say it → rewrite it.

title: Nobody Actually Wants Your Behind-the-Scenes Content
hook: Nobody wants your behind-the-scenes content. And every social media coach telling you to post it is wrong.
topic: BTS content without emotional stakes is self-serving content that doesn't convert — the real format that makes 'process' content work is conflict, tension, and resolution.

script.label: HOOK
script.text: Nobody wants your behind-the-scenes content. And every social media coach telling you to post it is wrong.

script.label: BODY 1
script.text: Here's what 'show your process' content looks like in practice. A video of you at your desk. A clip of your morning routine. Some drone footage of your shop. A reel of you packing orders. 'Day in the life!' Nobody stopped for any of that. Not because it's boring — because it's irrelevant. Your audience doesn't care about your process. They care about their own problem. And you just showed them yours.

script.label: REHOOK
script.text: Here's the thing...

script.label: BODY 2
script.text: BTS content is a permission slip business owners give themselves not to say anything interesting. It feels productive. It feels authentic. But if there's no emotional hook — no story — no lesson the viewer walks away with — it's just footage. You know what actually works? Behind-the-scenes WITH stakes. Not 'here's my desk.' 'Here's the email that almost ended my company — and what I did in the next 24 hours.' Not 'here's how we pack orders.' 'Here's the day we fulfilled our biggest order with half our team out sick.' Stakes. Tension. Resolution. That's the story. Without that — BTS is just content for content's sake.

script.label: BODY 3
script.text: Show your process. But only if the process has a problem in it. Because conflict is the story. And the story is what makes someone stop scrolling.

script.label: OUTRO
script.text: Next time you're about to post a 'day in the life' — ask yourself: what's the problem in this story? If there isn't one — don't post it. Find the problem. That's your content.

value: Polarizing take that will generate debate. Validates the gut feeling of business owners who post BTS and get zero response. Gives the real fix in one sentence.
cta: BTS without stakes = content for content's sake.
caption:
Hot take: nobody wants your behind-the-scenes content.

'Day in the life' videos. Morning routine clips. Packing order reels.

Nobody stopped for any of that.

Not because it's boring.
Because it's IRRELEVANT.

What actually works:

BTS with stakes.

❌ "Here's my desk"
✅ "Here's the email that almost ended my company"

❌ "Here's how we pack orders"
✅ "Here's how we fulfilled our biggest order with half the team out sick"

Conflict is the story.
No conflict → no content.

Fight me in the comments. ⬇️

title: Script This: Therapist
hook: Therapists think they have the most restricted content space of any profession. HIPAA. Ethics boards. Confidentiality. I wrote one a script that grew her practice — without naming a single client.
topic: Scripting a therapist using the 'name the block before the door' format — addressing the shame and hesitation that keeps prospects from booking, without ever touching client confidentiality.

script.label: HOOK
script.text: Therapists think they have the most restricted content space of any profession. HIPAA. Ethics boards. Confidentiality. I wrote one a script that grew her practice — without naming a single client.

script.label: BODY 1
script.text: Here's what most therapist content looks like. Stock photos of clasped hands on a wood desk. 'Are you struggling with anxiety?' in clean sans-serif. A three-step breathing guide. Completely clinical. Completely forgettable. The content is so cautious it says nothing. And when content says nothing — it converts nothing. Because nobody hires a therapist from a stock photo. They hire because they felt something. Because they thought: 'this person understands exactly what I'm going through.'

script.label: REHOOK
script.text: Here's the script I wrote—

script.label: BODY 2
script.text:
Hook: 'Most people who need therapy aren't afraid of the work. They're afraid of saying out loud that they need it.'

Body: 'There's a specific kind of person who fills out the intake form, then closes the browser. Who types the message to schedule — then deletes it. Who books a first appointment and cancels the morning of. If that's you — I want you to hear this: that hesitation is not weakness. It actually means you're taking it seriously. The people who struggle the most to reach out are often the ones who benefit the most from showing up. The first session is just a conversation. You don't have to have it figured out. Neither do I. Come talk.'

Then you add a link to schedule. No client stories. No private information. Just radical honesty about the block that's keeping someone out of the chair.

script.label: BODY 3
script.text: You're not violating anything by describing the emotional state of someone who hasn't called you yet. That's not a client — that's a prospect who doesn't know they need you. Name what they're feeling before they walk in the door. That's actually the most ethical content a therapist can make.

script.label: OUTRO
script.text: If you're a therapist, counselor, or coach and your content isn't converting — it's not your ethics. It's your strategy. Drop your practice type below. You might be next.

value: Expands Script This to the mental health/wellness industry — massive untapped category with massive content constraints. Proves the most ethically restricted professionals can still create deeply converting content. High save + share from therapists, counselors, and coaches.
cta: Name the block before they reach the door.
caption:
Therapists think they have the most restricted content space online.

I wrote one a script that grew her practice.
Without naming a single client.

Most therapy content: stock photos. Breathing tips. "Are you struggling?"
Clinical. Cautious. Converts nothing.

Name the emotional state of the person who HASN'T called you yet.

They fill out the intake form. Close the browser.
They book the first session. Cancel the morning of.

title: The Best Thing Your Content Can Do Is Repel the Wrong Client
hook: The best thing your content can do is make a bad client decide not to contact you. And most business owners don't know they can engineer that on purpose.
topic: How to engineer content that self-selects clients — specific positioning language that repels bad-fit buyers before they ever DM, and attracts ideal clients on autopilot.

script.label: HOOK
script.text: The best thing your content can do is make a bad client decide not to contact you. And most business owners don't know they can engineer that on purpose.

script.label: BODY 1
script.text: You've had that client. The one who haggles on price. Doesn't respect the process. Disappears for two weeks and needs it done by tomorrow. Thinks they know better than you. Calls at 9pm. By the time you're done with that client — you've lost money. Even if they paid the invoice. Now imagine that client landing on your Instagram and self-selecting OUT before they ever hit your DMs. That's what positioning content does. It's a filter. And almost nobody is building it intentionally.

script.label: REHOOK
script.text: Here's the thing...

script.label: BODY 2
script.text: When your content is specific enough — it attracts the right buyer AND repels the wrong one. Example: if you post 'we don't work with businesses that want quick results — we build for the long game' — the client who wants 30 days to go viral reads that and doesn't call. The client who's been burned by fast agencies reads that and calls immediately. Both of those are wins. One saved you time. One made you money. The most expensive client in any service business isn't the one who didn't pay — it's the one who was never a fit in the first place. Content that qualifies buyers before discovery eliminates the majority of those situations.

script.label: BODY 3
script.text: The goal isn't to attract everyone. It's to attract only the people who are a perfect fit — and make everyone else feel like you're not their match. That's not arrogance. That's efficient business.

script.label: OUTRO
script.text: Write one post this week that your worst client would read and think: 'this isn't for me.' That post will attract your best client. That's the system. Follow for more.

value: Hits a deep, rarely-named frustration — bad clients that drain time and money. Reframes content as a client filter, not just a marketing tool. Highly shareable among agency owners and freelancers who've been burned.
cta: Right client in. Wrong client out. That's the filter.
